title: Business-continuity rehearsal evidence — BCP/DR exercise execution logged
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate technical restore SLAs (covered by 22.45.1); focuses on human-orchestrated rehearsal.
evidence: Saved search 'bcp_rehearsal_evidence' (quarterly) emits BUs whose rehearsal is older than the policy cadence.

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.24 testing of digital operational resilience, explicitly including rehearsals.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that DORA Art.24 (Digital operational-resilience testing) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.45.5: Business-continuity rehearsal evidence — BCP/DR exercise execution logged.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.45.5' running on BCP rehearsal ticketing system, signed rehearsal attestations., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIS2 business continuity and crisis management.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) (Business continuity and crisis management) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.45.5: Business-continuity rehearsal evidence — BCP/DR exercise execution logged.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.45.5' running on BCP rehearsal ticketing system, signed rehearsal attestations., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST-800-53 CP-4 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.45.5: Business-continuity rehearsal evidence — BCP/DR exercise execution logged.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.45.5' running on BCP rehearsal ticketing system, signed rehearsal attestations., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A BU has not logged a rehearsal in 13 months (policy cadence: 12 months); the UC fires quarterly.
controlTest.negativeScenario: BU has logged a rehearsal within 12 months; no alert.

dataSources: BCP rehearsal ticketing system, signed rehearsal attestations.
spl:
| inputlookup business_units.csv
| lookup bcp_rehearsals.csv bu_id OUTPUT last_rehearsal_at signed_by
| eval days_since=round((now()-strptime(last_rehearsal_at,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where isnull(last_rehearsal_at) OR days_since>cadence_days
| table bu_id owner last_rehearsal_at days_since cadence_days
description: Moves BCP/DR rehearsal from a 'we did it' statement into a per-business-unit attestation log, mapped to DORA Art.24 and NIS2 Art.21(2)(c).
value: Converts a calendar item into evidence with a signed attestation per rehearsal.
implementation: (1) Maintain business_units.csv with owner + cadence; (2) emit bcp_rehearsals.csv after each exercise with a signed_by; (3) run quarterly.
visualization: Heatmap of BU × days-since-rehearsal, table of at-risk BUs, single-value 'BUs within cadence'.
knownFalsePositives: New BUs need an onboarding_date carve-out until the first scheduled rehearsal.
grandmaExplanation: Converts a calendar item into evidence with a signed attestation per rehearsal — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: BCP rehearsal ticketing system, signed rehearsal attestations..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain business_units.csv with owner + cadence; (2) emit bcp_rehearsals.csv after each exercise with a signed_by; (3) run quarterly.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup business_units.csv
| lookup bcp_rehearsals.csv bu_id OUTPUT last_rehearsal_at signed_by
| eval days_since=round((now()-strptime(last_rehearsal_at,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where isnull(last_rehearsal_at) OR days_since>cadence_days
| table bu_id owner last_rehearsal_at days_since cadence_days
```

Understanding this SPL

**Business-continuity rehearsal evidence — BCP/DR exercise execution logged** — Converts a calendar item into evidence with a signed attestation per rehearsal.

Documented **Data sources**: BCP rehearsal ticketing system, signed rehearsal attestations. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_since** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(last_rehearsal_at) OR days_since>cadence_days` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Business-continuity rehearsal evidence — BCP/DR exercise execution logged**): table bu_id owner last_rehearsal_at days_since cadence_days


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Heatmap of BU × days-since-rehearsal, table of at-risk BUs, single-value 'BUs within cadence'.
